subject: Computing
sub_strand: Generation of Computers and Parts of a Computer and Other Gadgets
content_standard: B6.1.1.1: Identify parts of a computer, technology tools and history of computers
indicator_description: Learn about the Generation of Computers
examplars:
1. Guide learners to discuss the Generation of Computers[cite: 575].
2. Microprocessors - Super Computers, Mainframe, Minicomputers and Microcomputers, Mobile Phones, etc[cite: 575].

subject: Computing
sub_strand: Generation of Computers and Parts of a Computer and Other Gadgets
content_standard: B6.1.1.1: Identify parts of a computer, technology tools and history of computers
indicator_description: Identify components of a Computer System: Hardware, Software and Liveware.
examplars:
1. Guide learners to identify Hardware (input, output, storage and communication), Software, Liveware and discuss the components and how they are used[cite: 575].
2. NB: the discussion should be limited to definition level[cite: 575].

subject: Computing
sub_strand: Generation of Computers and Parts of a Computer and Other Gadgets
content_standard: B6.1.1.1: Identify parts of a computer, technology tools and history of computers
indicator_description: Identify the left, right mouse button, holding of mouse, performing single, double and triple clicking, dragging object and using the scroll wheel.
examplars: 1. Guide learners to master the use of mouse through practical sessions and games[cite: 575].

subject: Computing
sub_strand: Generation of Computers and Parts of a Computer and Other Gadgets
content_standard: B6.1.1.1: Identify parts of a computer, technology tools and history of computers
indicator_description: Demonstrate proper use of keyboarding technique.
examplars: 1. Guide learners to type stories using Word Processor or typing tutorial (e.g. Mavis Beacon) to improve typing speed[cite: 575].

subject: Computing
sub_strand: Generation of Computers and Parts of a Computer and Other Gadgets
content_standard: B6.1.1.1: Identify parts of a computer, technology tools and history of computers
indicator_description: Summarise the generation of computers (second generation of computers.)
examplars:
1. Group learners into groups. Guide them to summarise the generation of computers[cite: 575].
2. After, let them present their findings in groups to the class. eg. First generation, Second generation, Third generation etc[cite: 575].

subject: Computing
sub_strand: Introduction to MS-Windows Interface (Desktop Background and Locations of the Computer)
content_standard: B6.1.2.1. Demonstrate the use of the Desktop Background as well as working with folders.
indicator_description: Explore the use of the desktop Background, changing the themes colours and User account. (e.g. classic, icons and Taskbar of the background)
examplars: 1. Guide learners to the use of the desktop Background, change the themes colours and the User account[cite: 578].

subject: Computing
sub_strand: Introduction to MS-Windows Interface (Desktop Background and Locations of the Computer)
content_standard: B6.1.2.1. Demonstrate the use of the Desktop Background as well as working with folders.
indicator_description: Demonstrate the use of the Recycle Bin or Trash Can.
examplars: 1. Guide learners to use the recycle bin by deleting and viewing deleted files[cite: 578].

subject: Computing
sub_strand: Introduction to MS-Windows Interface (Desktop Background and Locations of the Computer)
content_standard: B6.1.2.1. Demonstrate the use of the Desktop Background as well as working with folders.
indicator_description: Perform permanent delete or Empty Trash can and restoring files or icons in the recycle bin.
examplars: 1. Support learners to liken the Recycle bin and its use to the real-world office trash can[cite: 578].

subject: Computing
sub_strand: Introduction to MS-Windows Interface (Desktop Background and Locations of the Computer)
content_standard: B6.1.2.1. Demonstrate the use of the Desktop Background as well as working with folders.
indicator_description: Explore the use of Copy, Paste, Delete tools, the moving of folders by using the Desktop Pop-Up menu.
examplars: 1. Guide learners to the use of the Copy, Paste, Delete tools, the moving of folders by using the Desktop Pop-Up menu[cite: 578].

subject: Computing
sub_strand: Introduction to MS-Windows Interface (Desktop Background and Locations of the Computer)
content_standard: B6.1.2.1. Demonstrate the use of the Desktop Background as well as working with folders.
indicator_description: Illustrate the use of File Explorer window and locations of the computer through the file explorer.
examplars: 1. Guide learners to the use File Explorer window and locations of the computer through the file explorer[cite: 578].

subject: Computing
sub_strand: Introduction to MS-Windows Interface (Desktop Background and Locations of the Computer)
content_standard: B6.1.2.1. Demonstrate the use of the Desktop Background as well as working with folders.
indicator_description: Locate the hard drives, and other removable storage icons in the File explorer.
examplars: 1. Guide learners to locate the hard drives and other removable storage icons in the File explorer[cite: 578].

subject: Computing
sub_strand: Introduction to MS-Windows Interface (Desktop Background and Locations of the Computer)
content_standard: B6.1.2.1. Demonstrate the use of the Desktop Background as well as working with folders.
indicator_description: Use the File Explorer Ribbon (Home Ribbon only), that is, the use of the Clipboard and Organise tools of the Home Ribbon of the File Explorer, as well as the Open New Window and Close tools of the File Menu of the File Explorer.
examplars: 1. Guide learners to use the File Explorer Ribbon (Home Ribbon only); the use of the Clipboard and Organise tools of the Home Ribbon of the File Explorer, and Open New Window and Close tools of the File Menu of the File Explorer[cite: 582].

subject: Computing
sub_strand: Introduction to MS-Windows Interface (Desktop Background and Locations of the Computer)
content_standard: B6.1.2.1. Demonstrate the use of the Desktop Background as well as working with folders.
indicator_description: Demonstrate the use of the navigation Pane of the File Explorer to access different locations of the computer from the navigation pane.
examplars: 1. Guide learners to the use of the navigation Pane of the File Explorer to access different locations of the computer from the navigation pane[cite: 582].

subject: Computing
sub_strand: Introduction to MS-Windows Interface (Desktop Background and Locations of the Computer)
content_standard: B6.1.2.1. Demonstrate the use of the Desktop Background as well as working with folders.
indicator_description: Demonstrate the use of Frequent Folders Section of the File Explorer to access frequently used locations of the computer.
examplars: 1. Guide learners to explore the use of Frequent Folders Section of the File Explorer to access frequently used locations of the computer[cite: 582].

subject: Computing
sub_strand: Introduction to MS-Windows Interface (Desktop Background and Locations of the Computer)
content_standard: B6.1.2.1. Demonstrate the use of the Desktop Background as well as working with folders.
indicator_description: Demonstrate the use of Recent Files Section of the File Explorer to access frequently used files of the computer.
examplars: 1. Guide learners to explore the use of Recent Files Section of the File Explorer to access frequently used files of the computer[cite: 582].

subject: Computing
content_standard: B6.1.3.1. Demonstrate the use of Data and identify sources of data
indicator_description: Identify types of data. (Integers, double, characters, float etc.)
examplars: 1. Guide learners to identify and record data in the different forms[cite: 586].

subject: Computing
content_standard: B6.1.3.1. Demonstrate the use of Data and identify sources of data
indicator_description: Identify more sources of data and information e.g. internet (emailing, Skype, SMS etc.)
examplars: 1. Guide learners to discuss where one can get data and information[cite: 586].

subject: Computing
content_standard: B6.1.3.1. Demonstrate the use of Data and identify sources of data
indicator_description: Demonstrate sending and receiving information from other gadgets e.g. Bluetooth, Infrared, Radio, Fax, Telephones calls, SMS etc.
examplars: 1. Guide learners to mention or talk about where one can receive or send information[cite: 586].

subject: Computing
content_standard: B6.1.3.1. Demonstrate the use of Data and identify sources of data
indicator_description: Demonstrate basic manipulations on sample data e.g. arranging data in a matrix table, sorting and calculations etc.
examplars:
1. Guide learners to sort data in alphabetical order (increasing and decreasing order) and perform basic calculations such as multiplying, dividing and adding vales of sample data[cite: 586].
2. Learners can also be guided to generate age data from dates of birth[cite: 586].

subject: Computing
content_standard: B6.1.3.1. Demonstrate the use of Data and identify sources of data
indicator_description: Demonstrate how to manage users of the Desktop
examplars:
1. Guide learners to brainstorm how to manage users of the Desktop[cite: 586].
2. Use videos or pictures that show how to manage users of the Desktop. Facilitate learners to do same[cite: 586].

subject: Computing
content_standard: B6.1.3.1. Demonstrate the use of Data and identify sources of data
indicator_description: Demonstrate how to collect data (e.g. listening to radio, reading newspapers, interviews use of questionnaires etc.)
examplars: 1. Lead learners to collect data the community using simple questionnaire, surveys, observations, and record their findings data[cite: 589].

subject: Computing
content_standard: B6.1.3.1. Demonstrate the use of Data and identify sources of data
indicator_description: Demonstrate the use of the tools for collecting data.
examplars: 1. Bring tools or pictures of the tools for collecting data e.g. case studies, interviews, documents checklists, etc[cite: 589].

subject: Computing
content_standard: B6.1.3.1. Demonstrate the use of Data and identify sources of data
indicator_description: Demonstrate data interpretation by computing data to gain required information (e.g. Finding sum or grand total using Electronic Spreadsheet).
examplars: 1. Guide learners to make meanings out of data collected using tables, charts or Spreadsheet[cite: 589].

subject: Computing
content_standard: B6.1.3.1. Demonstrate the use of Data and identify sources of data
indicator_description: Demonstrate data presentation in different forms.
examplars: 1. Guide learners to write data in tables, pictures, charts, maps, words etc. as well as type them in a Spreadsheet[cite: 589].

subject: Computing
content_standard: B6.1.3.1. Demonstrate the use of Data and identify sources of data
indicator_description: Demonstrate how to store data.
examplars:
1. Guide learners to use the tools stated above to store data in different formats [cite: 589].
2. eg. handwriting, phone recording, magnetic tapes, optical disc etc[cite: 589].

subject: Computing
content_standard: B6.1.3.1. Demonstrate the use of Data and identify sources of data
indicator_description: Manipulate data to gain required output (e.g. Finding sum, mean, grand totals, maximum, minimum, mode, division, multiplication etc.)
examplars: 1. Guide learners to find the mode, product, minimum value, maximum value etc[cite: 592].

subject: Computing
content_standard: B6.1.4.1. Demonstrate the use of Technology in the community.
indicator_description: Define communication.
examplars: 1. Put learners into groups, to discuss communication and present findings to the class[cite: 595].

subject: Computing
content_standard: B6.1.4.1. Demonstrate the use of Technology in the community.
indicator_description: Identify three more technological tools for communication in the community.
examplars: 1. Guide learners to mention and describe the importance of technology in communication[cite: 595].

subject: Computing
content_standard: B6.1.4.1. Demonstrate the use of Technology in the community.
indicator_description: Demonstrate communication with others.
examplars: 1. Guide learners to demonstrate communication (explain how communication is done via TV, radio, phone call, satellite etc.)[cite: 595].

subject: Computing
content_standard: B6.1.4.1. Demonstrate the use of Technology in the community.
indicator_description: Identify three more importance of technology in communication.
examplars: 1. Guide learners to mention the importance of technology in communication[cite: 595].

subject: Computing
content_standard: B6.2.1.1 Demonstrate how to use Microsoft PowerPoint
indicator_description: Demonstrate how to use the File menu, the Insert and Design Ribbon from B5.
examplars: 1. Guide learners to use File menu, Insert and Design Ribbons[cite: 599].

subject: Computing
content_standard: B6.2.1.1 Demonstrate how to use Microsoft PowerPoint
indicator_description: Demonstrate how to use icons in the Text group in the Insert Ribbon.
examplars: 1. Guide learners to properly use the icons in the Insert Ribbon[cite: 599].

subject: Computing
content_standard: B6.2.1.1 Demonstrate how to use Microsoft PowerPoint
indicator_description: Be able to give a 5-side presentation in MS-PowerPoint using the tools of the ribbons studied.
examplars: 1. Guide learners to present their work to the class[cite: 599].

subject: Computing
content_standard: B6.3.1.1. Illustrate the use of word processing application
indicator_description: Demonstrate how to use the File menu and Insert, Design, and Layout Ribbons from B5.
examplars: 1. Guide learners to use File menu, Insert, Design and Layout Ribbon[cite: 604].

subject: Computing
content_standard: B6.3.1.1. Illustrate the use of word processing application
indicator_description: Demonstrate how to use icons in the Text group in the Insert Ribbon.
examplars: 1. Guide learners to properly use the icons in the Insert Ribbon[cite: 604].

subject: Computing
content_standard: B6.3.1.1. Illustrate the use of word processing application
indicator_description: Be able to use the attributes of the ribbons studied in a paragraph.
examplars: 1. Guide the learners to create and format text in a document[cite: 604].

subject: Computing
sub_strand: Introduction to Databases, Algorithm and Programming. Languages
content_standard: B6.5.1.1. Demonstrate how to manipulate data in Databases
indicator_description: Describe Databases.
examplars: 1. Guide learners through discussion in groups, to list and describe databases[cite: 612].

subject: Computing
sub_strand: Introduction to Databases, Algorithm and Programming. Languages
content_standard: B6.5.1.1. Demonstrate how to manipulate data in Databases
indicator_description: Identify databases and data structures.
examplars: 1. Guide learners to identify databases structures, ie. database structure is the collection of record type and field type definitions that comprises of the database[cite: 612].

subject: Computing
sub_strand: Introduction to Databases, Algorithm and Programming. Languages
content_standard: B6.5.1.1. Demonstrate how to manipulate data in Databases
indicator_description: Explain Fundamental Database concepts.
examplars: 1. Guide learners apply the fundamental database concepts to a sample database in a class discussion[cite: 612].

subject: Computing
sub_strand: Introduction to Databases, Algorithm and Programming. Languages
content_standard: B6.5.1.1. Demonstrate how to manipulate data in Databases
indicator_description: Describe the basics of Relational Data model.
examplars: 1. Lead learners to describe the basics of Relational Data model. ie. rows and columns[cite: 612].

subject: Computing
sub_strand: Introduction to Databases, Algorithm and Programming. Languages
content_standard: B6.5.1.1. Demonstrate how to manipulate data in Databases
indicator_description: Identify the basics of logical Database Design.
examplars: 1. Guide learners to list the basics of logical database design and develop sample database design[cite: 612].

subject: Computing
sub_strand: Introduction to Databases, Algorithm and Programming. Languages
content_standard: B6.5.1.1. Demonstrate how to manipulate data in Databases
indicator_description: Illustrate basics of relational algebra.
examplars: 1. Guide learners apply the basics of relational algebra in a sample database[cite: 612].

subject: Computing
sub_strand: Introduction to Databases, Algorithm and Programming. Languages
content_standard: B6.5.1.1. Demonstrate how to manipulate data in Databases
indicator_description: Operate basics SQL: querying and manipulating data.
examplars:
1. Guide learners to write simple queries to retrieve specific names of students from a database of the names of all students in class database design[cite: 615].
2. eg. CREATE DATABASE, SELECT and UPDATE[cite: 615].

subject: Computing
sub_strand: Introduction to Databases, Algorithm and Programming. Languages
content_standard: B6.5.1.1. Demonstrate how to manipulate data in Databases
indicator_description: Operate basics SQL: querying and manipulating data.
examplars:
1. Guide learners to write simple queries to retrieve specific names of students from a database of the names of all students in class [cite: 615].
2. e.g CREATE TABLE, DELETE and INSERT INTO[cite: 615].

subject: Computing
sub_strand: Introduction to Electronic Spreadsheet (Tabs and Ribbons Manipulation)
content_standard: B6.5.3.1. Demonstrate how to use Spreadsheet.
indicator_description: Demonstrate how to use the ribbons under the home ribbons. (i.e. clip board, styles, fonts, paragraph and editing)
examplars: 1. Guide learners to do a presentation on the use of the ribbons under the home button[cite: 619].

subject: Computing
sub_strand: Introduction to Electronic Spreadsheet (Tabs and Ribbons Manipulation)
content_standard: B6.5.3.1. Demonstrate how to use Spreadsheet.
indicator_description: Learners to create basic worksheets using Microsoft Excel 2016.
examplars: 1. Guide learners to develop worksheet consisting of list of names ie. teachers, students, friends, families, etc[cite: 619].

subject: Computing
sub_strand: Introduction to Electronic Spreadsheet (Tabs and Ribbons Manipulation)
content_standard: B6.5.3.1. Demonstrate how to use Spreadsheet.
indicator_description: Perform calculations in an MS-Excel worksheet.
examplars: 1. Guide learners to develop worksheet that will help in calculating multiplication, addition and subtraction[cite: 619].

subject: Computing
sub_strand: Introduction to Electronic Spreadsheet (Tabs and Ribbons Manipulation)
content_standard: B6.5.3.1. Demonstrate how to use Spreadsheet.
indicator_description: Modify an MS-Excel worksheet.
examplars: 1. Guide learners to modify worksheet by adding the age of learners in the class to the list of learners created in an earlier class[cite: 619].

subject: Computing
sub_strand: Introduction to Electronic Spreadsheet (Tabs and Ribbons Manipulation)
content_standard: B6.5.3.1. Demonstrate how to use Spreadsheet.
indicator_description: Modify the appearance of data within a worksheet.
examplars:
1. Show examples of modified data in (a) worksheet(s), either on projected screens or pictures[cite: 619].
2. Guide learners to modify the appearance of data within a worksheet[cite: 619].

subject: Computing
sub_strand: Introduction to Electronic Spreadsheet (Tabs and Ribbons Manipulation)
content_standard: B6.5.3.1. Demonstrate how to use Microsoft Excel
indicator_description: Manage Excel workbooks.
examplars: 1. Guide learners to properly name MS-Excel workbooks and store them in folders for retrieval later[cite: 622].

subject: Computing
sub_strand: Introduction to Electronic Spreadsheet (Tabs and Ribbons Manipulation)
content_standard: B6.5.3.1. Demonstrate how to use Microsoft Excel
indicator_description: Print the content of an MS-Excel worksheet.
examplars: 1. Guide learners to adjust margins to suit the A4 paper size for printing in landscape and portrait[cite: 622].

subject: Computing
content_standard: B6.6.1.1. Demonstrate how to Network computers.
indicator_description: Give examples of facilities the internet offers.
examplars: 1. Guide learners to give examples of internet facilities ie. e-mail, FTP's, www, etc. for presentation[cite: 626].

subject: Computing
content_standard: B6.6.1.1. Demonstrate how to Network computers.
indicator_description: Describe the types of information available on the Internet.
examplars:
1. Guide learners to identify and describe the types of Information available on the Internet. eg. Educational, Financial, entertainment, etc[cite: 626].
2. Group learners into groups to do this task[cite: 626].

subject: Computing
content_standard: B6.6.1.1. Demonstrate how to Network computers.
indicator_description: Identify data duplication.
examplars: 1. Display list of names of learners on the board and ask them to identify the duplicated data[cite: 626].

subject: Computing
content_standard: B6.6.1.1. Demonstrate how to Network computers.
indicator_description: Outline what one needs to connect to the Internet.
examplars: 1. Encourage learners to bring any device or pictures of devices that are needed. eg. desktop computers, Laptop computers, Mobile phones, etc[cite: 626].

subject: Computing
content_standard: B6.6.2.1. Demonstrate the use of a Web Browser
indicator_description: Identify the address or links window.
examplars: 1. Guide learners to identify the address or links window on phones other electronic gadgets with browsers[cite: 630].

subject: Computing
content_standard: B6.6.2.1. Demonstrate the use of a Web Browser
indicator_description: Recognise the status bar and list its use.
examplars: 1. Bring a picture of a window that has the status bar and ask the learners to point out the status and mention its functions[cite: 630].

subject: Computing
content_standard: B6.6.2.1. Demonstrate the use of a Web Browser
indicator_description: Illustrate using help button.
examplars:
1. Guide learners to do an activity you have not tackle in class and ask them to use the help button to navigate and find the steps involved[cite: 630].
2. Guide learners to carry out the stated steps[cite: 630].

subject: Computing
content_standard: B6.6.2.1. Demonstrate the use of a Web Browser
indicator_description: Recognise internet explorer speed keys.
examplars: 1. Ask learners to use the speed keys to open a new browser, make font size bigger in a browser, etc[cite: 630].

subject: Computing
content_standard: B6.6.2.1. Demonstrate the use of a Web Browser
indicator_description: Recognise Uniform Resource Locators (URLs).
examplars:
1. Guide learners to discuss the properties of URLs, their uses and where they are located[cite: 633].
2. Learners can practise how to enter URLs in a browser[cite: 633].

subject: Computing
content_standard: B6.6.2.1. Demonstrate the use of a Web Browser
indicator_description: Demonstrate how to return to a URL.
examplars: 1. Guide the learners to navigate using the back button to go to a previous page[cite: 633].

subject: Computing
content_standard: B6.6.2.1. Demonstrate the use of a Web Browser
indicator_description: Show how to find items on a page.
examplars: 1. Guide learners to search for items using the search textbox[cite: 633].

subject: Computing
content_standard: B6.6.3.1. Demonstrate Surfing The World Wide Web
indicator_description: Recognize resource locators (URLs).
examplars:
1. Guide learners to identify what URLs is[cite: 636].
2. Group learners into groups of five or less, to explore on how to recognise URLs so as to aid learners to locate a resource on the web[cite: 636].

subject: Computing
content_standard: B6.6.3.1. Demonstrate Surfing The World Wide Web
indicator_description: Illustrate how to jump directory to URLs.
examplars: 1. Give learners a project on how to jump directory to URLs[cite: 636].

subject: Computing
content_standard: B6.6.3.1. Demonstrate Surfing The World Wide Web
indicator_description: Demonstrate how to return to a URL.
examplars: 1. Guide learners to investigate how to return to a URL[cite: 636].

subject: Computing
content_standard: B6.6.3.1. Demonstrate Surfing The World Wide Web
indicator_description: Show how to find items on a page.
examplars: 1. Guide learners to discuss on how to find items on a page. Guide learners present their ideas or findings to class[cite: 636].

subject: Computing
content_standard: B6.6.3.1. Demonstrate Surfing The World Wide Web
indicator_description: Illustrate how to print pages.
examplars: 1. Guide learners on how to print pages e.g. selected pages, only selected pages etc[cite: 636].

subject: Computing
content_standard: B6.6.4.1. Demonstrate searching for information on the Web.
indicator_description: Show how to create a favourite link.
examplars: 1. Guide learners to create an Internet favourite link[cite: 640].

subject: Computing
content_standard: B6.6.4.1. Demonstrate searching for information on the Web.
indicator_description: Demonstrate deleting favourite links.
examplars: 1. Guide learners to delete a favourite link they have created[cite: 640].

subject: Computing
content_standard: B6.6.4.1. Demonstrate searching for information on the Web.
indicator_description: Create favourite folder.
examplars:
1. Guide learners to create a favourite folder or subfolder[cite: 640].
2. Help learners by aiding them through the necessary steps to create a favourite folder[cite: 640].

subject: Computing
content_standard: B6.6.4.1. Demonstrate searching for information on the Web.
indicator_description: Use the links toolbar and Favourite Places.
examplars: 1. Guide learners to use the links toolbar[cite: 640].

subject: Computing
content_standard: B6.6.5.1. Demonstrate the Usage of Forms
indicator_description: Demonstrate the types and uses of form elements.
examplars: 1. Explore the uses of check boxes, radio buttons, textboxes etc[cite: 644].

subject: Computing
content_standard: B6.6.5.1. Demonstrate the Usage of Forms
indicator_description: Demonstrate the filing of forms offline.
examplars: 1. Guide learners to open and save a page. Lead them to fill the forms offline[cite: 644].

subject: Computing
content_standard: B6.6.5.1. Demonstrate the Usage of Forms
indicator_description: Demonstrate uploading of files.
examplars: 1. Lead learners to explore the upload button by adding pictures, audio, pdf etc[cite: 644].

subject: Computing
content_standard: B6.6.5.1. Demonstrate the Usage of Forms
indicator_description: Illustrate retrieving text, pictures, sounds and programs.
examplars: 1. Lead learners to explore the download button by downloading pictures, audio, pdf etc[cite: 644].

subject: Computing
content_standard: B6.6.5.1. Demonstrate the Usage of Forms
indicator_description: Discuss more about security on the form submission.
examplars: 1. Guide learners to Investigate and identify security issues when typing account details online (Antivirus, Credit card details, personal passwords etc.)[cite: 644].

subject: Computing
content_standard: B6.6.6.1. Show how to Customize a Browser
indicator_description: Identify reasons for customising a web browser.
examplars: 1. Discuss the reasons needed for customising an item (i). to gain access to quick commands or information (ii) for side by side browsing (iii) for beautifications[cite: 648].

subject: Computing
content_standard: B6.6.6.1. Show how to Customize a Browser
indicator_description: Illustrate how to set themes.
examplars: 1. Explore on customisation, locate the more settings, Under appearance[cite: 648].

subject: Computing
content_standard: B6.6.6.1. Show how to Customize a Browser
indicator_description: Show how to customise the toolbar.
examplars: 1. Lead learners to explore in customising a browser... e.g. themes, wallpaper, tools etc[cite: 648].

subject: Computing
content_standard: B6.6.6.1. Show how to Customize a Browser
indicator_description: Set bookmarks.
examplars: 1. Demonstrate the meaning of bookmarks - Bookmarks, makes you save shortcuts to your favourite webpages and navigate to them in seconds from anywhere[cite: 648].

subject: Computing
content_standard: B6.6.6.1. Show how to Customize a Browser
indicator_description: Show how to set cookies demonstrate the steps in turning on cookies in a browser.
examplars:
1. Guide learners to turn on cookies in a browser[cite: 648].
2. From the Tools menu, select Internet Options and enable session cookies, click the Privacy tab[cite: 648].
3. From the Settings section of the tab, click Advanced[cite: 648].
4. Locate and click the checkbox next to Always allow session cookies[cite: 648].

subject: Computing
content_standard: B6.6.7.1. Demonstrate the use of Electronic Mail
indicator_description: Show how to create and access e-mail messages.
examplars: 1. Guide learners to receive email[cite: 653].

subject: Computing
content_standard: B6.6.7.1. Demonstrate the use of Electronic Mail
indicator_description: Illustrate how to reply to and forward received messages.
examplars: 1. Guide learners to use forward and reply buttons in email[cite: 653].

subject: Computing
content_standard: B6.6.7.1. Demonstrate the use of Electronic Mail
indicator_description: Demonstrate how to delete messages.
examplars: 1. Guide leaners to delete email messages[cite: 653].

subject: Computing
content_standard: B6.6.7.1. Demonstrate the use of Electronic Mail
indicator_description: Illustrate filing of e-mail messages.
examplars: 1. Guide learners to create folders in the Inbox and sorting emails into them[cite: 653].

subject: Computing
content_standard: B6.6.7.1. Demonstrate the use of Electronic Mail
indicator_description: Create address list.
examplars: 1. Guide learners to create an address list[cite: 653].

subject: Computing
content_standard: B6.6.7.1. Demonstrate the use of Electronic Mail
indicator_description: Demonstrate attaching files to e-mail messages.
examplars: 1. Guide learners to attach files to email[cite: 653].

subject: Computing
content_standard: B6.6.7.1. Demonstrate the use of Electronic Mail
indicator_description: Demonstrate some e-mail tips.
examplars:
